channel.title: Hummingbird on PrestaShop Developer Documentation
channel.description: Recent content in Hummingbird on PrestaShop Developer Documentation

item.title: CSS conventions
item.description: CSS conventionsHummingbird’s CSS is built on Bootstrap 5.3, organized in modular SCSS with CSS @layer for cascade control and BEM naming for custom components. Bootstrap 5.3Hummingbird is built on Bootstrap 5.3. Use Bootstrap utilities and components when they fit; extend with custom BEM-named components when they don’t. Illustrative example: <!-- Bootstrap utilities: layout, spacing, no custom class needed --> <div class="d-flex justify-content-between p-3">...</div> <!-- Custom BEM: theme-specific structure that Bootstrap doesn't cover --> <div class="product-card product-card--featured"> <h3 class="product-card__title">.

item.title: JavaScript conventions
item.description: JavaScript conventionsHummingbird’s JavaScript is written in TypeScript, compiled with Webpack, and follows modern patterns: no jQuery in theme code, semantic data-ps-* attributes for DOM targeting, and a centralized event system. No jQueryjQuery is loaded by core.js for backward compatibility with existing modules. Theme code must not use jQuery. Write vanilla JavaScript (or TypeScript) using modern ECMAScript syntax. Semantic data-ps-* attributesHummingbird decouples styling from behavior by introducing semantic data-ps-* attributes instead of CSS classes as JavaScript hooks:

item.title: Accessibility
item.description: AccessibilityAccessibility is a baseline requirement in Hummingbird, not an optional enhancement. It is already mandatory in many countries (for example under the European Accessibility Act) and is increasingly treated as a standard for e‑commerce. The theme is built to comply with EAA-relevant standards. All theme contributions must maintain or improve accessibility compliance. See Accessibility requirements for a detailed checklist that applies to every PrestaShop theme. What the theme implementsThe theme puts a strong focus on accessibility.

item.title: Development workflow
item.description: Development workflow You need Node.js 20.x and npm to build Hummingbird. For the full list of tools, see Requirements. For setting up a local PrestaShop instance, see Environment setup. Script commands Command Purpose npm ci Install exact dependency versions from the lockfile npm run build Compile SCSS and TypeScript into production assets npm run watch Watch for file changes and recompile automatically npm run lint Run ESLint and Stylelint npm run format Run Prettier npm run storybook Launch Storybook component explorer Run npm ci first to install dependencies, then npm run build to compile.

item.title: Bootstrap compatibility
item.description: Bootstrap compatibility Why this mattersHummingbird uses Bootstrap 5.3, but the PrestaShop module ecosystem was largely built on Bootstrap 4 (Classic theme). Bootstrap 5 introduced breaking changes: renamed classes, namespaced data attributes (data-bs-*) and the removal of jQuery as a dependency. Without intervention, modules that rely on BS4 patterns render incorrectly or lose interactive behavior (modals, tooltips, dropdowns) when used with Hummingbird. The Bootstrap Compatibility LayerThe Bootstrap Compatibility Layer is a standalone library, it is not part of Hummingbird.
